name: Lance of Longinus
description: The Lance of Longinus, also known as the Holy Lance or Spear of Destiny, is a legendary weapon claimed to have pierced the side of Jesus Christ during his crucifixion. Its association with immense power and divine judgment has made it a highly sought-after artifact throughout history and a potent symbol in religious and secular contexts. The legend states that the Roman soldier Longinus used the lance to ensure Christ's death, an act that subsequently led to Longinus's conversion to Christianity. The lance is said to grant invincibility and control over destiny to its wielder, but also carries a curse of unimaginable destruction if wielded improperly or by someone of impure intent. Numerous historical figures, including emperors and conquerors, have allegedly possessed the lance, believing it to be the key to their success. Its current whereabouts are unknown, fueling endless speculation and archaeological pursuits. The lance remains a powerful symbol of faith, power, and the enduring legacy of the crucifixion narrative.
subtitle: The spear that pierced Christ's side.
origin: Created by a Roman blacksmith and used by the soldier Longinus to pierce the side of Jesus Christ during the crucifixion.
currentLocation: Unknown. Various locations claimed, including Vienna and Rome.

keyMyths.title: The Piercing of Christ
keyMyths.description: According to the Gospel of John, Roman soldiers broke the legs of the crucified criminals to hasten their deaths. However, seeing that Jesus was already dead, they did not break his legs, but one of the soldiers pierced his side with a spear. This act fulfilled a prophecy and provided the blood and water that flowed from the wound, symbolizing the sacraments of the Church. The spear became a relic immediately.

keyMyths.title: Longinus's Conversion
keyMyths.description: Tradition holds that the Roman soldier Longinus, who pierced Jesus' side, was blind or had poor eyesight. When the blood of Christ splashed into his eyes, his sight was miraculously restored. This event led to Longinus's immediate conversion to Christianity and his subsequent martyrdom for his faith. He is now venerated as a saint in the Catholic Church.

keyMyths.title: The Spear and Destiny
keyMyths.description: Legends surrounding the Lance of Longinus developed over centuries, claiming that whoever possessed the spear held the fate of the world in their hands. This belief led numerous historical figures to seek the lance, believing it granted them invincibility and the power to conquer. The spear became a symbol of ultimate power and a dangerous object of obsession.

sources.author: Robert Feather
sources.work: The Mystery of the Copper Scroll of Qumran: The Secret of the Temple Treasures
sources.description: Explores ancient artifacts and their historical significance, providing context for understanding the Lance's symbolic power.
sources.text: Explores ancient artifacts and their historical significance, providing context for understanding the Lance's symbolic power.

sources.author: Richard Abanes
sources.work: End-Time Visions: The Road to Armageddon?
sources.description: Analyzes the enduring fascination with apocalyptic prophecies and symbols, including the Lance of Longinus, in popular culture and religious belief.
sources.text: Analyzes the enduring fascination with apocalyptic prophecies and symbols, including the Lance of Longinus, in popular culture and religious belief.

sources.author: Trevor Ravenscroft
sources.description: Presents a controversial and speculative account of the Lance of Longinus and its alleged influence on historical events, particularly the rise of Nazi Germany.
